summary.intent: Add a `fetch_frames` convenience function to the signalkit library that combines fetching and parsing, plus a CLI entry point, tests, and a git commit.
summary.outcome: All deliverables were completed: `fetch_frames` added to `client.py`, CLI added as `__main__.py`, exports updated in `__init__.py`, 3 new tests added, all 10 tests passed, and changes committed as `2bc197b`.

repo: Library uses only the Python standard library — no third-party dependencies
Retry behavior is encapsulated in the existing `fetch` function and must not be altered when composing higher-level helpers
`__all__` in `signalkit/__init__.py` is the canonical export list and must be updated when adding public API

code.finding: `fetch_frames` is a thin composition: calls `fetch(url, **kwargs)` then `parse_frame` on the result, so retry logic stays in `fetch` unchanged
CLI entry point prints each key=value pair from the parsed frame dict; invoked via `python3 -m signalkit <url>`
Three new tests cover: parsed dict result, empty body edge case, and `**kwargs` forwarding to the underlying `fetch`

workflow: Reading all relevant files before editing allowed parallel edits to client, init, and a new __main__.py in one pass
Tests were run before committing to validate all 10 pass
